title: Gymnocalycium gibbosum
intro: Gymnocalycium gibbosum, commonly known as the Pincushion Cactus, is a type of cactus native to Mexico that belongs to the Cactaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, burns, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnocalycium gibbosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Gymnocalycium gibbosum is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This classification is part of the larger order Caryophyllales and class Magnoliopsida within the kingdom Plantae. Further classification places it in the subclass Rosidae, and the clade Asterids, and the order Caryophyllales. It is a member of the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Cacteae. The genus Gymnocalycium is a group of cacti that are native to Mexico and the southwestern United States.
